topic: Toronto summer festival season — which ones to attend

turns.content: SUMMER IS COMING and I need to figure out which festivals to hit up this year!! Toronto festival season is about to go CRAZY and I can't miss out
Toronto summers really are packed with festivals! Are you looking to attend as a fan, or are you also thinking about performing at some of them? That changes the strategy a lot.

BOTH!! like some I wanna attend just for the vibes and networking but a couple I'd love to actually get booked for. I'm a DJ and producer so anything with electronic or bass music or Caribbean sounds is my LANE

Great, that gives us a lot to work with. For your style, here are some key ones to look at:

**To attend / network:**
- **VELD Music Festival** (July/August) -- big EDM and electronic focus, good for seeing what's trending and networking with other producers
- **Afrofest** (usually July at Woodbine Park) -- one of the largest free African music festivals in North America, amazing for Caribbean and Afro-diaspora connections
- **Jerk Festival** -- Caribbean food and music, great crowd energy

**To try to get booked:**
- **Digital Dreams** -- if it's running this year, it's electronic-focused and books emerging local talent
- **TD Toronto Jazz Festival** -- they've been expanding into electronic and experimental acts
- **Manifesto Festival** -- hip-hop and urban arts, they actively support local artists

Have you submitted to any festival lineups before?
Afrofest is a MUST I went last year and the energy was unmatched!! and yeah I've played a couple smaller stages at festivals before but nothing huge. I really want to level up this summer still

Afrofest is incredible for connecting with the Caribbean and African music community. Since you've done smaller stages, you're not starting from zero which is good.

For leveling up, here's what I'd suggest:

1. **Start submitting NOW** -- most festival lineups are being booked between March and May. You're right on time but don't wait.
2. **EPK (Electronic Press Kit)** -- make sure you have a solid one: bio, high-quality photos, mix recordings or live set videos, social media links, press mentions. Festivals want to see you're professional and can draw a crowd.
3. **Target the mid-tier stages** -- don't go straight for the main stage. The second or third stages at bigger festivals are actually better for building your name.
4. **Local promoter relationships** -- a lot of festival bookings come through promoters who already know you from club nights. Are you connected with any Toronto promoters?

I know a few promoters from doing club nights yeah!! and my EPK is decent but honestly I should update the photos, the ones I have are from like a year ago. what about Caribana weekend tho?? that's the biggest one for me culturally

Caribana weekend (Toronto Caribbean Carnival) is MASSIVE -- that whole first weekend of August the city transforms. But here's the thing about Caribana: the official parade and main events have their own booked acts, but the REAL opportunity is the dozens of unofficial parties and events that happen around it.

During Caribana weekend:
- Every club, rooftop, and venue in the city is throwing events
- Promoters bring in big acts but also need local DJs for warmup sets and afterparties
- Boat cruises, pool parties, block parties -- there's so many slots to fill

If your sound has Caribbean and dancehall elements, Caribana weekend should be your #1 priority. Start reaching out to the promoters you know NOW and let them know you're available for that weekend. Even if you don't get a headline spot, doing 2-3 events during Caribana weekend is incredible exposure.

YO the boat cruise ting is SO fire I did one two years ago and it was the best set I ever played!! something about DJing on the water with the Toronto skyline behind you is just DIFFERENT. I need to lock in at least one boat party for sure

Boat cruise sets are legendary! The energy is unreal because people literally can't leave -- they're stuck on the boat so the dance floor stays packed the whole time. And the sunset backdrop with the skyline? That's content gold too.

For locking one down:
- Find out which companies ran boat parties last Caribana weekend and reach out directly
- Offer a competitive rate since the exposure is worth it
- Ask if they need an opening DJ -- those 5-7pm sunset slots are actually the most fun
- Make sure you have a USB backup of your set, boat sound systems can be unpredictable

Also, film EVERYTHING during that set. Get someone with a decent phone to capture clips for your socials. Festival bookers look at how you engage a crowd just as much as your track selection.

facts FACTS!! ok so my summer plan is: update EPK and photos in March, submit to festival lineups in April, lock in Caribana weekend events by June, hit Afrofest for networking in July. the RIDDIM of this summer is going to be INSANE

That's a solid timeline! One more thing to add:

- **May/June** -- go to a few smaller events just to be SEEN. Show up, network, take photos with other artists and promoters. Being present in the scene reminds people you exist when they're filling lineups.

And update those EPK photos ASAP -- spring is a great time for outdoor shoots in Toronto before the summer rush. Get some shots that show your personality and your DJ setup.

You've got a real plan now. This summer could be a big one for you if you stay on the timeline. Good luck with the submissions!
BLESS!! thank you for helping me map this out. I was just excited about summer but now I actually have a STRATEGY. time to make moves!!

That's the difference between hoping for opportunities and creating them! You've got the talent and the connections, now you've got the plan too. Get that EPK updated first -- everything else flows from there. Have an amazing festival season!
